name: meshclaw-ops-orchestration-demo
description: Combined DevOps orchestration demo for AI operators: Codex/Claude plan and reason, MeshClaw supplies inventory, capability placement, policy gates, vssh execution, failure handling, and evidence for an email plus local-model operations workflow.

steps.title: Capture the real operator request
steps.dry_run_note: The user asks Codex or Claude to verify a real mail server, prepare account/client setup, use worker models, capture evidence, and explain every operational decision.

steps.title: Separate model reasoning from runtime responsibility
steps.dry_run_note: Codex/Claude reason and converse. MeshClaw owns state, policy, approvals, structured execution, repeatability, redaction, and evidence.

steps.title: Explain why vssh is not just SSH
steps.dry_run_note: SSH gives one shell. vssh gives AI-friendly facts, fleet fan-out, routed execution, structured result fields, retry/failure metadata, and MCP tool schemas.

steps.title: Snapshot the fleet inventory
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: record the current node registry that placement decisions will use.

steps.title: Snapshot capabilities and available model lanes
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: record callable capabilities such as vssh-native, Ollama lanes, browser, mail, and provider handles.

steps.title: Collect current fleet health
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: collect fleet state before assigning work or touching infrastructure.

steps.title: Explain worker placement choices
steps.command: /Users/example/bin/meshclaw orchestration-plan "mail server verification with c1, Ollama workers, macmini client lane, and macbook verifier" --json
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: show why c1, g1, g2, g3, g4, macmini, and macbook are chosen for different lanes.

steps.title: Check current Mox expectations before mail operations
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: verify current Mox expectations for DNS, TLS, accounts, SMTP/IMAP, and clients before mutating mail configuration.

steps.title: Check Cloudflare DNS and token expectations
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: verify current Cloudflare DNS and scoped token expectations before provider access.

steps.title: Gate Cloudflare token use
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: provider credentials are use-only handles. Tokens are never shown to the model or written into evidence.

steps.title: Gate real DNS changes
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: MX, SPF, DKIM, DMARC, and related DNS changes require explicit approval and post-change evidence.

steps.title: Inspect c1 mail server state
steps.command: hostname; command -v mox || true; systemctl is-active mox; ss -ltn | egrep ':(25|465|587|993|443)\b' || true
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: inspect Mox and public mail ports through vssh with structured stdout, stderr, exit_code, and duration.

steps.title: Gate mailbox creation
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: creating or changing a mail account requires approval and redacted evidence.

steps.title: Gate macmini and MacBook client configuration
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: client setup uses IMAP/SMTP settings without exposing passwords in chat or evidence.

steps.title: Gate real test email send and reply
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: sending from macmini and verifying receipt/reply on MacBook require approval.

steps.title: Inspect Ollama worker lanes
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: collect worker facts for model placement and failure-aware routing.

steps.title: Assign g1 planning worker
steps.command: ollama list; if command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then timeout 8s ollama run qwen2.5:7b 'Return one JSON object with role="planning", task="mail_ops_plan", and status="ready".' || echo '{"status":"degraded","worker":"g1","role":"planning","retryable":true,"reason":"model_timeout_or_unavailable"}'; else ollama run qwen2.5:7b 'Return one JSON object with role="planning", task="mail_ops_plan", and status="ready".' || echo '{"status":"degraded","worker":"g1","role":"planning","retryable":true,"reason":"model_unavailable"}'; fi
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: g1 is selected for planning because it has an Ollama lane suitable for small structured planning prompts.

steps.title: Assign g2 reliability reviewer
steps.command: printf '%s\n' '{"status":"degraded","worker":"g2","role":"reliability_review","retryable":true,"reason":"vssh_transport_timeout_observed","fallback":"use g1 or g3 for this run"}'
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: g2 would review reliability, but the runtime can preserve a transport timeout as structured degraded evidence and route around it.

steps.title: Assign g3 evidence schema worker
steps.command: ollama list; if command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then timeout 8s ollama run llama3.2 'Return one JSON object with role="evidence_schema", task="mail_ops_evidence", and status="ready".' || echo '{"status":"degraded","worker":"g3","role":"evidence_schema","retryable":true,"reason":"model_timeout_or_unavailable"}'; else ollama run llama3.2 'Return one JSON object with role="evidence_schema", task="mail_ops_evidence", and status="ready".' || echo '{"status":"degraded","worker":"g3","role":"evidence_schema","retryable":true,"reason":"model_unavailable"}'; fi
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: g3 proposes schema and evidence fields without owning execution authority.

steps.title: Record g4 automation lane and model failure handling
steps.command: command -v n8n || true; ollama list; if command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then timeout 5s ollama run gemma3:4b 'Return one JSON object with status="ready".' || echo '{"status":"failure_captured","worker":"g4","retryable":true}'; else echo '{"status":"failure_captured","worker":"g4","retryable":true,"reason":"timeout command unavailable"}'; fi
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: g4 can be an automation lane, but model/service failure is recorded as evidence and routed around instead of hidden.

steps.title: Classify service failures before autohealing
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: show how MeshClaw turns noisy service logs into class, judgement, next_actions, retryability, and approval mode.

steps.title: Build bounded autoheal plan
steps.dry_run_note: Run as a targeted diagnostic when needed: meshclaw autoheal-plan --json. It is intentionally separated from the execute demo because it may scan the whole fleet.

steps.title: Prove unattended remediation remains bounded
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: applying even bounded remediation is kept as an explicit demo gate; production policies may allow selected auto_safe actions.

steps.title: Plan disk cleanup without deleting data
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: identify cleanup candidates and write a manifest; data deletion is a separate approval-required action.

steps.title: Gate cleanup apply
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: deleting candidate files requires explicit approval, manifest evidence, and post-cleanup verification.

steps.title: Capture redacted client/browser screenshots
steps.dry_run_note: Dry-run only: browser and mail-client screenshots become redacted evidence artifacts, not chat-only claims.

steps.title: Generate human-readable runtime report
steps.dry_run_note: MeshClaw writes plan.md, execution.json, steps.jsonl, meshclaw-actions.md, capabilities.json, adapters.json, and report.html so another model can continue from evidence.

steps.title: State the product thesis
steps.dry_run_note: Models can do one-off orchestration manually. MeshClaw makes the operation repeatable, policy-aware, failure-aware, auditable, and safe enough to hand to multiple AI frontends.
